rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verify capabilities before assert.
Known correct Claude Code facts — transcript path format.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Student-visible body (line 32) uses '~/.claude/projects/<encoded>/' correctly. Maintainer artefact table uses '<project-folder>' (imprecise vs canonical '<encoded-cwd>') but lives below <!-- maintainer --> and is not student-facing.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Transcript-by-mtime selection fragile; marker-based selection required.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module body (line 32) references M4 session transcript via fallback to 'SHA Claude reported at Module 4 close' — marker-based, not mtime-only. Artefact contract table documents <session-id>.jsonl correctly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-working-directory boundaries always open new.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Line 34: 'Open a new Claude Code session in the worktree' — correctly uses new, not return/resume, for the cross-cwd worktree boundary.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Sessions are the unit of work — use 'start/open a new session,' not 'open Claude Code in X.'
rules_evaluated.evidence: Lines 26, 34 use 'open a fresh session' and 'Open a new Claude Code session' — names the session explicitly, not just the app.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source .md files use bare paths; depth-counted ../../ prefixes are forbidden.
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:10: `[Clean Code Is Steering: Insights from Uncle Bob](../../trainings/agentic-engineering-101/supplementary/clean-code-is-steering.md)` — depth-counted prefix added in commit 46b5947. Renderer accepts it but convention forbids it (curriculum/CLAUDE.md § Cross-doc links; compounded/2026-04-26-platform-bare-paths-renderer-rewrites.md).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Auto-load is filename-based, not folder-based.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Module correctly describes CLAUDE.local.md and .claude/memory/ as files that must be explicitly copied at fork time, not auto-loaded. No claims of automatic cross-worktree propagation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source URL + type label mandatory; labels in maintainer block only.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Source-verification maintainer block present. Prework citations use correct attribution: 'Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen' ([practitioner analysis]). Klaassen's own articles labeled [practitioner direct, vendor venue]. Labels live below <!-- maintainer --> only; body uses builder voice.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Freshness 6 months.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Source-verification block explicitly includes 'freshness re-check before first cohort' for each practitioner citation. All four URLs sampled alive as of 2026-05-15.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Zombie stat guard.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No round-number AI failure/success stats in student-facing body. The specific '19.2% auto-approved' number (Curran/Intercom) lives in the maintainer source-verification block with explicit flag as vendor-self-reported.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block on student-facing files with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Block present at lines 156–161 with: every URL to open, numbers to verify (19.2%), named fallback framings, freshness re-check instructions for each source. Compliant with all four §11 requirements.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No placeholders inside fenced blocks.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Angle-bracket templates (<repo-name>, <slug>) appear in ae101-m5-worktree-setup prompt body. Rule 1 targets [BRACKETS] square-bracket placeholders. Angle-bracket forms are derive-from-context instructions Claude resolves from the active working directory at runtime; body line 28 confirms 'Claude figures out <repo-name> from the working directory.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompt markers have action lead-ins in the module body: worktree-setup is preceded by 'Claude figures out <repo-name>...' framing; rerun-packaged is preceded by 'Ask Claude to re-run the same Module 4 task using the reference, plan.md, and verifier you just built.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path, not invented alias.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-rerun-packaged: 'The reference, plan.md, and verifier are at the paths we wrote in this session' — scrollback-review chain. Rule 5 explicitly prefers transcript-as-authoritative-memory for chains; no deterministic-path violation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight in Claude Code.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Neither ae101-m5-worktree-setup nor ae101-m5-rerun-packaged registry bodies contain **bold** or *italic* markup inside the fenced content.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Preamble before action — structural trigger at 4+ sub-steps requires suppressor or body primer.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-worktree-setup prompt (expanded) has 6 named sub-steps: find branch, validate log, spin worktree, cp CLAUDE.local.md, mkdir .claude/, cp memory/. No in-fence 'No preamble.' suppressor. No body primer immediately after {{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}} naming preamble + skim-past cue. The bolded callout that follows ('The grep may hit multiple branches.') serves the rule-33 fallback, not a preamble primer.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Add body primer after {{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}}: 'Claude will narrate before acting; skip past the opening and look for the worktree path and file-copy confirmation.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strong-bold prompt label is exactly **Prompt** — custom labels break the renderer.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Expanded output shows both prompts render with canonical **Prompt** *(Claude Code)* label shape. Registry frontmatter `dest: Claude Code` correctly drives the chip.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Prompts live in registry; module files reference by marker.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Both prompts use registry markers {{prompt:ae101-m5-worktree-setup}} and {{prompt:ae101-m5-rerun-packaged}}. Registry files exist at curriculum/prompts/ with correct frontmatter (key, dest, runtime, origin). expand-md.js resolves both cleanly.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No module-name references in prompt prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-worktree-setup registry body line 9: 'so M5/M6 compounding has the M1/M3 evidence to build on' — four bare M-number references (M5, M6, M1, M3) in prose outside backticks, double-quotes, or path segments. Exemptions (branch names in code spans, commit messages in double-quotes) do not apply.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rewrite to reference artefacts: e.g. 'so the verifier-build and re-send have the personal-rules and memory evidence from earlier sessions to build on.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Git-output greps that assert exact-N need multi-result fallback.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-worktree-setup fence includes 'There may be more than one' + log-validation instruction. Body callout after the marker ('The grep may hit multiple branches.') provides the rule-33 option-A fallback with specific recovery steps. Correctly implemented.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Disambiguation and decision logic belong in the prompt fence, not body callouts.
rules_evaluated.evidence: ae101-m5-worktree-setup prompt fence carries the full disambiguation logic for multi-branch case inline. Body callout mirrors the explanation for the student but does not carry logic the fence omits.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Installed skills invoke by name, never by path.
rules_evaluated.evidence: No skill invocations inside fenced prompt blocks. 'Ralph re-feed' appears as a concept name in LOs and leap test (body text only), not as a [Skill: ralph-loop] prompt invocation.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions live in prompts, not body prose.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Phase-level forcing functions for the exercise live in diagnose-and-resend.md (exercise file). The two prompts in this module file encode their own forcing functions inside fenced blocks.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Verification machinery needs smoke-test.
rules_evaluated.evidence: Verifier is built and tested in Phase 3 of the exercise before the re-send prompt chains on it. Per-phase failure-mode table names verifier-shape mismatch and escape hatch.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Context-loading tiers — auto-load assumptions checked.
rules_evaluated.evidence: CLAUDE.local.md and .claude/memory/ correctly described as requiring explicit copy at fork time. No auto-load claims across worktree boundaries.

platform_claims_checked.claim: gitignored files (CLAUDE.local.md, .claude/memory/) don't ride into a worktree because git doesn't see them
Open a new Claude Code session in the worktree. The rest of Module 5 runs there.

platform_claims_checked.note: Depth-counted path works at runtime (renderer accepts (?:\.\.\/)*) but violates bare-path convention. Bare path: trainings/agentic-engineering-101/supplementary/clean-code-is-steering.md

platform_claims_checked.claim: M5 session transcript at ~/.claude/projects/<project-folder>/<session-id>.jsonl (maintainer artefact table)
platform_claims_checked.note: Maintainer-block only; uses '<project-folder>' (imprecise vs canonical '<encoded-cwd>') but does not reach students.

citations_checked.claim: Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen, 'You're the Bread in the AI Sandwich' (every.to)
Kieran Klaassen, 'Compound Engineering: The Definitive Guide' (every.to)
Kieran Klaassen, 'My AI Had Already Fixed the Code Before I Saw It' (every.to)
Darragh Curran / Intercom — 2x Nine Months Later (ideas.fin.ai); 19.2% auto-approved flagged as vendor-self-reported

notes: File was modified at commit 46b5947 (2026-05-15) after the technical@ audit SHA (0f32201, 2026-05-14). The prework line change (body only, no prompt block) added the depth-counted path violation (finding #6 above) and a writing-surface change; per per-class auto-degrade rules, writing@ is degraded but technical@ is degraded only if a platform-relevant change occurred. The depth-counted path is a platform-convention violation introduced by that commit, so technical@ should be re-pinned after the bare-path fix is landed.
